name: Salad of Pomegranates, Haricots Verts, Jicama and Walnuts
recipeIngredient: 3/4 pound haricot verts (thin french green beans)
1 tablespoon chopped fresh flat-leafed parsley leaves
recipeInstructions:
1. Using a manual citrus juicer, squeeze enough juice from pomegranate halves to measure 1/4 cup. Press the sides against the center of the juicer and press whole seeds into the juicer with your thumbs.
2. Peel jícama and cut into 1/4-inch-thick slices. On a cutting board, stack slices in groups of two or three and cut into 1/4-inch-thick sticks.
3. In a bowl, toss the jícama with pomegranate juice. Chill the mixture, covered, tossing occasionally, for 30 minutes to allow the jícama to absorb some of the juice.
4. Have ready a bowl of ice and cold water. Trim haricot verts and blanch in boiling salted water for 3 minutes, or until crisp-tender. Transfer beans with a slotted spoon to ice water to stop cooking and drain in a colander.
5. Cut remaining pomegranate into halves. Bend back the rinds and dislodge seeds from membranes. Coarsely chop walnuts.
6. To the jícama mixture, add blanched haricot verts, pomegranate seeds, chopped parsley, olive oil, lemon juice, salt, and pepper. Toss to combine.
7. Serve the salad sprinkled with walnuts.
